description: Reference prompt set for LLM-as-a-Judge zero-shot quality validation (Section 2.3) and Model Performance Profiling (Section 3.1). 15 prompts across 5 discipline groups (3 per discipline) with varying class counts.

prompts.message: Create a course outline for a second-year undergraduate course on data structures and algorithms. Cover arrays, linked lists, trees, graphs, sorting, and searching. Emphasize time complexity analysis.
Design a course outline for an operating systems course. Include process management, memory management, file systems, and concurrency. Target third-year CS students.
Create an introductory machine learning course for students with basic Python and statistics knowledge. Cover supervised and unsupervised learning, evaluation metrics, and a capstone project.
Create a first-semester organic chemistry course outline. Cover nomenclature, functional groups, reaction mechanisms (substitution, elimination, addition), stereochemistry, and spectroscopy basics.
Design a cell biology course for second-year biology students. Cover cell structure, membrane transport, cell signaling, the cell cycle, and gene expression. Include lab-related activities.
Create a thermodynamics course for engineering students. Cover the laws of thermodynamics, energy balance, entropy, thermodynamic cycles, and real-world applications to engines and refrigeration.

prompts.topic: Modern Philosophy: From Descartes to Nietzsche
prompts.message: Create a course outline on modern Western philosophy. Cover rationalism, empiricism, Kantian ethics, existentialism, and the works of Descartes, Hume, Kant, Hegel, and Nietzsche. Emphasize primary text analysis.

Design a world literature course covering major 20th-century movements: modernism, postmodernism, magical realism, and postcolonial literature. Include authors from at least 4 continents. Focus on close reading and critical essays.

Create an introductory macroeconomics course. Cover GDP, inflation, unemployment, fiscal and monetary policy, international trade, and economic growth models. Include real-world data analysis exercises.

Create a research methods course for social science students. Cover qualitative and quantitative approaches, survey design, interviews, statistical analysis basics, and research ethics. Students should complete a mini research proposal.

Design a social psychology course covering social cognition, attitudes, conformity, group dynamics, prejudice, aggression, and prosocial behavior. Include classic experiment analyses and applied scenarios.

Create a course on public policy analysis. Cover policy design, stakeholder analysis, cost-benefit analysis, implementation challenges, and evaluation. Use real policy case studies from healthcare and education.

Design a DSP course for electrical engineering students. Cover discrete-time signals, Z-transform, DFT/FFT, digital filter design (FIR and IIR), and applications in audio and communications. Include MATLAB/Python exercises.

Create a structural mechanics course for civil engineering students. Cover stress and strain, beam bending, torsion, combined loading, column buckling, and failure theories. Include problem-solving sessions.
Design a robotics course covering kinematics, dynamics, sensors, actuators, path planning, and basic control systems. Target third-year engineering students. Include hands-on robot programming labs.
